summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes the Multi-Teacher Distillation Pretraining (MTDP) framework, which replaces traditional self-supervised masked reconstruction with knowledge distillation from cross-modal foundation models. The method employs a two-stage process: first, a learnable gating network fuses representations from DINOv3 (vision) and Chronos (time-series) using a masked latent denoising objective; second, this fused representation is distilled into a student EEG model. Extensive evaluations across 12 datasets and 9 tasks demonstrate that MTDP outperforms self-supervised counterparts while requiring only 25% of the pretraining data.

headline_results: Outperformed self-supervised CBraMod on 10/12 datasets at 100% data
Outperformed self-supervised CBraMod on 9/12 datasets using only 25% pretraining data

key_points: New EEG foundation model pretraining framework: MTDP leverages cross-modal priors from DINOv3 and Chronos to overcome the data scarcity and low signal-to-noise ratio of EEG signals.
Two-stage distillation process: Uses a learnable gating network for unsupervised teacher fusion followed by cosine-similarity-based distillation into a student model.
High data efficiency: Outperforms state-of-the-art self-supervised EEG models on 9 out of 12 downstream tasks using only 25% of the pretraining data.
limitations: Increased data processing and inference costs compared to masked reconstruction due to the use of large teacher models.
Additional hard disk and I/O burdens resulting from the need to store pre-computed teacher representations.

method.architecture: Student: CBraMod (criss-cross transformer). Teachers: DINOv3-ViT-B/16 and Chronos-Bolt-Base.
method.finetuning: Jointly tuning the backbone and a 3-layer MLP classifier on downstream tasks.
method.objective: Two-stage: (1) Masked latent denoising for teacher fusion; (2) Cosine similarity for knowledge distillation.
method.pretraining: Multi-Teacher Distillation Pretraining (MTDP) using a learnable gating network to fuse cross-modal representations.

summary.one_liner: A pretraining framework for EEG foundation models that distills knowledge from vision and time-series foundation models via a two-stage gating and distillation process.
summary.title: Standing on the Shoulders of Giants: Rethinking EEG Foundation Model Pretraining via Multi-Teacher Distillation
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces the MTDP framework to bootstrap EEG foundation models by distilling complementary representations from established vision and time-series foundation models.

papers.title: Standing on the Shoulders of Giants: Rethinking EEG Foundation Model Pretraining via Multi-Teacher Distillation

reasons: Explicitly proposes a new pretraining framework (MTDP) specifically for EEG foundation models.
Focuses on creating reusable EEG representations by distilling knowledge from other modality foundation models.
Demonstrates broad transferability and utility across 9 downstream tasks and 12 datasets.

summary.detailed_summary: Laya proposes a self-supervised learning framework that predicts latent embeddings of masked temporal EEG segments rather than reconstructing raw signals. It incorporates a montage-agnostic channel mixer and explicit geometric regularization (SIGReg) to ensure stable training and prevent representation collapse. Evaluation on EEG-Bench shows that Laya-S, trained on only 10% of the pretraining data, outperforms larger reconstruction-based models on clinical tasks and demonstrates superior robustness to additive noise.

headline_results: Laya-S achieved 0.597 mean balanced accuracy on clinical tasks, outperforming LaBraM (0.573) and LUNA (0.560).

key_points: New EEG foundation model: Laya utilizes a LeJEPA-based latent prediction objective to learn transferable representations without the noise bias of signal reconstruction.
Demonstrates high sample efficiency, with a version trained on 10% of the data (Laya-S) achieving superior mean balanced accuracy on clinical tasks compared to LaBraM and LUNA.
Shows significant resilience to noise and captures clinically meaningful temporal structures, such as seizure onset, as evidenced by PCA visualization.
limitations: Scaling dynamics are unclear, as Laya-full performed slightly worse than Laya-S.
Evaluation is limited to classification tasks, excluding retrieval or forecasting.
Linear probing may underestimate the model's utility for non-linear tasks.
BCI performance remains limited, suggesting a need for subject-level adaptation.
Lacks explicit support for bipolar montages and intracranial electrodes.

method.architecture: Transformer encoder with RoPE and a lightweight Transformer predictor; uses a 1D convolutional patch embedder and a query-based cross-attention channel mixer.
method.finetuning: Linear probing (frozen encoder) on 14 tasks from EEG-Bench.
method.objective: Masked latent prediction using MSE between predicted and target embeddings, combined with Sketched Isotropic Gaussian Regularization (SIGReg).
method.pretraining: Self-supervised pretraining on 29,109 hours of EEG data across multiple datasets.

summary.one_liner: Laya is the first EEG foundation model based on LeJEPA, replacing signal reconstruction with latent prediction to learn noise-resilient, semantically rich representations.
summary.title: Laya: A LeJEPA Approach to EEG via Latent Prediction over Reconstruction
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces the first application of LeJEPA to EEG, utilizing latent prediction and isotropic Gaussian regularization to avoid the bias toward high-variance artifacts inherent in reconstruction-based objectives.

papers.title: Laya: A LeJEPA Approach to EEG via Latent Prediction over Reconstruction

reasons: Explicitly identifies Laya as the first EEG foundation model based on LeJEPA
Focuses on learning transferable representations from large unlabeled corpora
Aims to improve upon existing EEG foundation models by replacing reconstruction objectives with latent prediction
Evaluates the model's effectiveness across a range of EEG benchmarks

summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes SpecMoE, a foundation model that employs a novel Gaussian-smoothed masking strategy on short-time Fourier transform (STFT) maps to prevent spectral leakage and artificial transients during self-supervised pretraining. It introduces SpecHi-Net, a U-shaped hierarchical architecture with dual-path convolutions and Global Transformers, and a spectral gating mechanism that routes signals to specialized experts based on Power Spectral Density (PSD). Evaluation across nine human and murine EEG datasets demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in tasks like seizure detection and drug effect prediction, showing strong cross-species generalization.

headline_results: SOTA in murine drug decoding (MACO) and human seizure detection (Siena)

key_points: New EEG foundation model: SpecMoE uses Gaussian-smoothed STFT masking and a hierarchical U-Net-like backbone to learn robust neural rhythms without boundary artifacts.
Novel spectral gating: Employs a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture where a learned gating mechanism routes inputs based on Power Spectral Density (PSD).
Cross-species efficacy: Achieves state-of-the-art results on 7 of 9 benchmarks, including significant gains in murine drug effect decoding and human seizure detection.
limitations: Requires full-parameter fine-tuning for optimal performance; zero-shot generalization remains an open challenge.
The 50% masking ratio was adopted from prior work and may be suboptimal.
Expert specialization in the MoE framework currently relies on stochastic data partitioning.

method.architecture: SpecHi-Net (U-shaped hierarchical encoder-decoder with dual-path convolutions and Global Transformers with RoPE)
method.objective: Self-supervised masked reconstruction of STFT maps using Gaussian-smoothed masks

summary.one_liner: A spectral-anchored EEG foundation model utilizing Gaussian-smoothed STFT masking and a PSD-guided Mixture-of-Experts framework for cross-species decoding.
summary.title: SpecMoE: Spectral Mixture-of-Experts Foundation Model for Cross-Species EEG Decoding
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces a Gaussian-smoothed time-frequency masking scheme and a PSD-guided Mixture-of-Experts framework to learn physiologically grounded EEG representations across species.

papers.title: SpecMoE: Spectral Mixture-of-Experts Foundation Model for Cross-Species EEG Decoding

reasons: Explicitly identifies as a foundation model for generalized EEG decoding.
Utilizes self-supervised pretraining with a novel masking scheme to learn reusable neural patterns.
Demonstrates broad transferability across diverse tasks and cross-species (human and murine) EEG datasets.

summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes FEMBA, a bidirectional Mamba architecture pre-trained on over 21,000 hours of EEG using a novel low-pass filtering reconstruction objective to prioritize neural oscillations over high-frequency artifacts. To enable edge deployment, the authors utilize Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) to compress the model to 2-bit weights, overcoming the activation outlier issues typical of State-Space Models (SSMs). The framework is successfully deployed on a GAP9 RISC-V microcontroller, achieving real-time inference with significantly lower FLOPs and memory footprint than Transformer-based benchmarks.

headline_results: 1.70s inference for 5s window on GAP9 MCU

key_points: New EEG foundation model: FEMBA uses a bidirectional Mamba architecture pre-trained on 21,787 hours of EEG data.
Physiologically-aware pre-training with low-pass filtering improves TUAB AUROC to 0.893, outperforming contrastive baselines.
Achieves 2-bit weight quantization via QAT, enabling deployment on GAP9 MCU with 1.70s inference per 5s window and ~2MB memory footprint.
limitations: Evaluation limited to clinical adult recordings from TUEG, TUAB, TUAR, and TUSL.
Inference is performed on fixed, non-overlapping 5s windows rather than a fully streaming regime.
Quantization analysis primarily focused on the FEMBA-Tiny architecture.
Lack of prospective clinical validation and assessment of user comfort or workflow integration.
The selective SSM scan remains a sequential compute bottleneck on the GAP9 hardware.

method.architecture: Bidirectional Mamba encoder with a lightweight Transformer decoder (used only for pre-training).
method.finetuning: Linear probing and full fine-tuning on TUAB, TUAR, and TUSL datasets.
method.objective: Physiologically-aware reconstruction using a low-pass filter (40 Hz cutoff) to prioritize neural oscillations.
method.pretraining: Pre-trained on TUEG using a masked reconstruction task with a low-pass filtered target.

summary.one_liner: A bidirectional Mamba-based EEG foundation model featuring physiologically-aware pre-training and 2-bit quantization for ultra-low-power MCU deployment.
summary.title: FEMBA on the Edge: Physiologically-Aware Pre-Training, Quantization, and Deployment of a Bidirectional Mamba EEG Foundation Model on an Ultra-low Power Microcontroller
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces a physiologically-aware low-pass reconstruction objective and a QAT-based 2-bit quantization framework to deploy a Mamba-based EEG foundation model on ultra-low-power microcontrollers.

papers.title: FEMBA on the Edge: Physiologically-Aware Pre-Training, Quantization, and Deployment of a Bidirectional Mamba EEG Foundation Model on an Ultra-low Power Microcontroller

reasons: Explicitly presents FEMBA, which is described as a bidirectional Mamba EEG foundation model.
Pre-trained on a large-scale dataset of over 21,000 hours of EEG for broad representation.
Focuses on the quantization and deployment ecosystem for EEG foundation models on ultra-low power hardware.

summary.detailed_summary: LuMamba addresses the challenges of electrode heterogeneity and computational scalability by fusing LUNA's cross-attention channel unification with FEMBA's bidirectional Mamba blocks. It introduces the first adaptation of the Latent-Euclidean Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture (LeJEPA) to biosignals, combining it with masked reconstruction to balance latent structure and cross-montage transferability. Pre-trained on over 21,000 hours of EEG, the model demonstrates state-of-the-art performance in Alzheimer's detection and significant efficiency gains over Transformer-based architectures.
key_points: New EEG foundation model: LuMamba combines topology-invariant encoding with linear-complexity bidirectional Mamba blocks for efficient, montage-agnostic modeling.
Adapts the LeJEPA framework to EEG, showing that a hybrid objective of distributional regularization and masked reconstruction improves robustness to distribution shifts.
Achieves 377x fewer FLOPS than LaBraM and supports 12x longer sequences before reaching memory limits while maintaining competitive clinical performance.
limitations: Underperforms task-specific state-of-the-art methods on TUAR and TUSL.
The generalization-oriented pre-training objective is less effective on highly class-imbalanced datasets like TUSL.
Downstream evaluation is limited to a small number of disease-specific datasets for mental condition classification.

method.architecture: LUNA embedder and channel unifier (cross-attention) followed by FEMBA bidirectional Mamba blocks.
method.objective: Hybrid of masked reconstruction and LeJEPA (combining SIGReg and JEPA predictive loss).

summary.one_liner: A computationally efficient EEG foundation model combining topology-invariant channel unification with bidirectional Mamba blocks and a hybrid LeJEPA-reconstruction objective.
summary.title: LuMamba: Latent Unified Mamba for Electrode Topology-Invariant and Efficient EEG Modeling
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces a topology-invariant SSM-based framework for EEG that integrates LeJEPA to regularize the latent space, improving generalization across heterogeneous electrode configurations.

papers.title: LuMamba: Latent Unified Mamba for Electrode Topology-Invariant and Efficient EEG Modeling

reasons: Explicitly frames the work as addressing challenges in building EEG foundation models.
Pre-trained on a large-scale unlabeled dataset of over 21,000 hours of EEG.
Evaluated on five diverse downstream tasks to demonstrate the generalizability of the learned representations.
Utilizes a self-supervised framework designed for broad transfer across different electrode configurations.

summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes Q-DIVER, which integrates the pretrained DIVER-1 EEG encoder with a Quantum Time-series Transformer (QTSTransformer) as a readout head. It employs Differentiable Quantum Architecture Search (DiffQAS) to autonomously optimize the quantum circuit topology during end-to-end fine-tuning. Evaluation on the PhysioNet Motor Imagery dataset shows that the quantum head achieves predictive performance comparable to a classical MLP while using 50x fewer task-specific parameters.

headline_results: 50x reduction in head parameters compared to MLP

key_points: Method for quantum transfer learning: Combines the DIVER-1 foundation model with a differentiable quantum classifier (QTSTransformer) for EEG decoding.
Autonomous architecture discovery: Uses DiffQAS to search 576 circuit configurations, discovering that parametric entangling gates (CRX) are optimal for EEG data.
Extreme parameter efficiency: Achieves a Test F1 of 63.49% on PhysioNet-MI using 50x fewer head parameters (2.10M) than a classical MLP (105.02M).
limitations: Evaluation is limited to a single downstream dataset (PhysioNet-MI).
Quantum circuits are simulated classically; performance on actual NISQ hardware is not yet demonstrated.
The search space for DiffQAS is relatively small (576 combinations).
Dependence on the DIVER-1 pretrained weights for performance.

method.architecture: Hybrid architecture using DIVER-1 (Transformer encoder) as the backbone and QTSTransformer as the head.
method.finetuning: End-to-end fine-tuning of both the DIVER-1 encoder and the quantum readout head.
method.objective: End-to-end classification loss with Differentiable Quantum Architecture Search (DiffQAS) for circuit optimization.
method.pretraining: DIVER-1 was pretrained using masked reconstruction of time-domain and spectral patches.

summary.one_liner: A hybrid EEG framework combining the DIVER-1 foundation model with a differentiable quantum classifier to achieve parameter-efficient motor imagery classification.
summary.title: Q-DIVER: Integrated Quantum Transfer Learning and Differentiable Quantum Architecture Search with EEG Data
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces a differentiable quantum architecture search framework to optimize the readout head of a pretrained EEG foundation model for parameter-efficient transfer learning.

papers.title: Q-DIVER: Integrated Quantum Transfer Learning and Differentiable Quantum Architecture Search with EEG Data

reasons: Utilizes a 'large-scale pretrained EEG encoder (DIVER-1)' as a core component.
Focuses on adaptation and fine-tuning of a pretrained EEG representation via quantum transfer learning.
EEG is the central modality for the biological signal processing task.
